evidence.page_title: Is This Horse Living in a Third-Floor Apartment? | Snopes.com
evidence.title: Is This Horse Living in a Third-Floor Apartment?
evidence.description: A horse needs a house, of course, of course.
evidence.text:
A photograph of a white horse standing at the window on the third floor of a brick building has long been circulated via social media, along with the claim that a tenant was discovered to have been keeping the equine in his apartment:
This picture has also become the subject of numerous humorous image macros, including the infamous "Soon" meme:
But this enigmatic horse was not spotted in Preston, Lancashire, or in many of the other locations attached to sightings of it. As documented by Google Street View, this horse was "residing" in Galena, Illinois (a town known for its well-preserved, 19th-century buildings and the home of Ulysses S. Grant just prior to the outbreak of the Civil War in 1861:
Moreover, this horse does not "live" with a tenant in a Galena apartment. As more close-up photographs have revealed, the equine is simply a life-size statue, similar to ones seen here.
https://www.shutterbug.com/content/white-horse-window

evidence.page_title: Did Mr. Ed Take Up Residence in an Apartment? | Snopes.com
evidence.title: Did Mr. Ed Take Up Residence in an Apartment?
evidence.description: Featured on Snopes today: Questions about a white horse in the window, a burned tree with a spaghetti pattern, and a woman flogged in Iran for drinking beer.
evidence.text:
In one of the weirder fact checks we've investigated, a photograph appears to reveal a white horse staring longingly out of a third-story apartment window, as if wishing to join passersby below. It’s been making the rounds on social media for years. The claim behind the photo was that the tenant was keeping the animal in his apartment.
David Mikkelson is hot on the case.
The Daily Debunker delivers top stories from Snopes.com to your inbox. Subscribe.

image_captions: A close up photo of Costco Wholesale branding on a store. The top half of the photo is a blue sky covered by out-of-focus leaves close to the camera

Screenshot of Facebook post. Has a photo of a young, brown-haired woman wearing glasses beneath text saying Hedviga Golik passed away alone in her apartment in 1966, and that her body went undiscovered for 42 years until found in 2008 during renovations
A black and white photo shows a nurse wearing a white coat looking at a baby in a wire cage attached to a window with blankets covering the bottom looking at them.
A tiger named Ming was found in a Harlem apartment in 2003 in this photo and the picture does not show the alligator that was also found.
